feature__info_action_summary: The toggles control the public visibility of the feature:
feature__info_intangible_feature: Feature is shown on the map if its layer is active. Set true if, in ordinary circumstances, the feature is accessible to the public without restrictions or permission. If the feature is temporarily inaccessible, this should be false.
feature__info_accesible: Feature may be shown on the map with the caveat that there is (no longer) a physical presence on site. This invites visitors to experience the feature's practical, historic context - e.g. sites of historic events; a striking contrast with the present; or a locality that is more salient than the missing materiality of the feature.
feature__info_action_note: Note that is a feature is permanently unavailable AND there is no historic, intangible value to referencing the site, then it should simply to unpublished to delist it from all maps.
feature__info_classifition_summary: Note that is a feature is permanently unavailable AND there is no historic, intangible value to referencing the site, then it should simply to unpublished to delist it from all maps.
feature__info_specification_summary: Free-form text fields that provide additional context and references. Fields are defined at the Collection level.
feature__info_map_summary: Unlock the map by clicking on the top-right lock, then drag the map to relocate the feature marker.

maps__projects_none_with_n_organisations: All projects for {n} organisations
slow_lower_wallaby_offer: Points are grouped together in Layers. You can toggle their visibility in the Layers section below.
alert_suave_moth_glow: Each layer is part of a Project and offered by an Organisation. If you are interested in a specific one, select them in the Organisations and Projects sections below. The Layer section will now only show what you've filtered for.
slow_quiet_dolphin_seek: Tip: Breadcrumbs above a layer name show you the project and organisation it belongs to.
bad_honest_pigeon_borrow: Use filters to only show points you are interested in. Select Neighbourhoods or filter by any of the Categories available in the layers.
watery_cuddly_niklas_harbor: You can search for region, district, or neighbourhood name. We also show the number of points available per neighbourhood for your active layers.
arable_mild_snake_pick: Layers add collections of points to the map. Filters select points from those layers, and hide the rest.
drab_many_gibbon_fulfill: If you select a neighbourhood from search, this highlights and focuses points in the neighbourhood. Filters only remove points that don't match your filters.
such_tense_samuel_smile: Use Stars to track the places you want to visit and have visited.
neat_watery_wasp_buy: Tap ☆ Star in the bottom left corner of any place card to add it to your list. Tap is again to remove it.
same_lower_orangutan_enchant: Tap ✓ Check In in the bottom left corner of any place card to record your visit.
extra_yummy_midge_wish: We use Google to authenticate you. Your name, profile picture, and password are managed through Google, and cannot be changed here.
petty_steep_dove_clasp: The app is currently available in English, Traditional, and Simplified Chinese.
sleek_main_shell_taste: We would love to acknowledge your contributions to the map. So please provide the name to be credited. This can either be your legal name, an organisation, or a nickname.
trite_key_insect_tickle: When you open the app, the map will load with these default Layers enabled.
big_awake_grebe_seek: Preview new features before they are released.
settings_language_auto_translate_descrip: If content is unavailable in your language(s), automatically translate it to your preferred language.
settings_language_show_translate_button_: Shows a button to translate or show the original text for descriptions
settings_language_prefer_placeholders_de: Shows placeholder text like "Unknown" instead of machine-translating content that's unavailable in your language(s)
settings_default_map_description: Choose which maps should load by default
settings_contributor_description: What name would you like to be credited for your contributed photos and features

validation__attribution_required: Please provide name to be credited.
validation__at_least_one_image: Please add at least one photo.

photo_credit_ensure_right_to_share: Only upload photos you have the right to share.
photo_credit_curatorial_review: Curators review photos before they are published.

report_missing__initial_instruction: Let them know why it's missing
report_missing__add_photo_instructions: Add a photo to help verify your report
weird_great_barbel_gaze: Other than rejecting the report, there are three actions you can take to change the feature's state:
tired_quick_snake_enjoy: Feature will no longer be shown on the public map, AND will be removed from the Admin panel. Take this action if visiting this feature is no longer of public interest, as it has physically been removed. In effect, this unpublishes the feature, and makes it unavailable in the admin panel.
great_sad_rook_cure: Feature will no longer be shown on the public map, BUT will still be visible in the Admin panel. Take this action, either because (1) visiting this feature is (temporarily) not possible; or (2) the maintainer wishes to keep the feature in the collection, but remove it from the public map. In effect, this sets the feature as inaccessible and unpublished.
flat_direct_bobcat_praise: Feature should be intangibalised only if its presence on the public map can provide practical, historic context for the feature - places where e.g. historic events took place; there's a striking contrast with the present; or the locality is more salient than the missing materiality of the feature. In effect, this marks a feature as intangible, but neither removes it from the public map, nor from the Admin panel.
strong_next_zebra_quiz: Would you like to accept or reject this user-submitted Feature?
awful_real_newt_lift: The feature will no longer appear anywhere.
careful_inner_grebe_skip: You will be redirected to the feature detail page where you can edit the user-submitted information, add translations, and further polish the Feature before it is published on the public map.
few_sunny_wren_push: Would you like to accept or reject this user-submitted photo to the Feature's gallery?
great_loose_gull_assure: None of the submitted photos will appear anywhere.
mild_deft_larva_absorb: The photos will all become publicly visible as part of the Feature's gallery. It will be sorted by photographic intent (Canonical, CloseUp, Context, General, Evidence, Undefined), then by date added.
blue_novel_owl_stab: Only the photos which have been asigned a photographic intent other than "undefined" will become publicly visible as part of the Feature's gallery. The rest will not appear anywhere.
happy_acidic_donkey_seek: Reworked images can be dropped into the viewer to replace the user-submitted image. The icon row in the bottom provides:
calm_away_panther_propel: The thumbnail gallery is a list of all images in the Feature's gallery. The photographic intent (e.g. Canonical, Context, etc) can be clicked to set another intent.
last_front_toucan_type: Any good story needs a protagonist.
lucky_dark_larva_express: Back to superhero school with you.

neat_noble_okapi_blink: Not a maintainer of this project
neat_noble_okapi_edit: Sudo can only when su does

active_nice_cougar_cook: If you remove yourself as owner or member, you will lose edit or access rights respectively
swift_weary_mule_persist: You can only update your own user
level_seemly_moose_intend: Shows placeholder text like "Unknown" instead of machine-translating content that\'s unavailable in your language(s)
awful_loved_shrimp_cuddle: Content is shown in this language by default. If it's not unavailable, we might machine translate depending on your settings.
chunky_giant_worm_feast: If the content is only available in (one of) your other language(s), we will show that instead of offering to machine translate it.
drab_lost_dove_grow: These settings control how and whether machine translation is used.
crazy_just_octopus_stop: automatically translates content to your preferred language. This means the full app experience will be in your preferred language, but the translation quality will be lower.
long_least_baboon_blend: gives you case-by-case control over whether machine-translation is used, and you can flip back and forth between the translation in your preferred language and the original text.
bland_honest_crossbill_amuse: shows placeholder text like "Unknown" or "Pending Translation" instead of machine-translating the content.
